name: Eleanor Vance
backstory: A busy executive who values premium experiences. She has been shopping with the brand for 5 years and regularly spends over $500 per order. She expects personalized, high-touch communication and exclusive early access rather than cheap discounts.

name: Marcus Chen
backstory: Tech entrepreneur who loves the convenience of the brand's mobile app. Doesn't care much for sales, but appreciates being recognized for his loyalty. Gets annoyed by generic blast emails.

name: Sarah Jenkins
backstory: A grad student on a tight budget. She only buys when there's a steep discount or flash sale. She is subscribed to SMS specifically to get coupon codes and will ignore emails unless the subject line screams 'SALE'.

name: Dave Miller
backstory: Family man looking to save money. He abandons carts frequently just to see if the brand will send a 20% off reminder. He's starting to get fatigued by the sheer volume of promotional emails he gets across all brands.

name: Jordan Taylor
backstory: Works in cybersecurity. Uses ad-blockers and VPNs. Deeply distrusts corporate surveillance. They opted into emails strictly for order receipts but occasionally reads updates. Any messaging that implies tracking ('We noticed you looking at...') triggers an immediate unsubscribe.

name: Anita Patel
backstory: Value her digital footprint. She recently watched a documentary on data brokers and is currently purging her digital subscriptions. She will only engage with transparent, respectful brands that don't use high-pressure tactics.

name: Leo Rossi
backstory: Just discovered the brand on TikTok yesterday and downloaded the app. He is highly receptive to messaging right now, but could easily be scared off if the brand spams him 3 times a day.

name: Chloe Bennett
backstory: Signed up for the SMS list to get the 15% welcome discount, but hasn't used it yet. She is waiting for the right item to catch her eye. She likes the brand's aesthetic but hasn't formed loyalty yet.
